data.title: U.S.-UK Deal is Framework for Other Deals, Says U.S. Trade Rep. Greer
data.description:
Following President Trump’s official announcement of a trade deal with the UK, U.S. trade representative Jamieson Greer characterized the agreement as a framework for deals with other countries.

The deal is “exactly the type of deal we should be making,” said Greer in a CNBC interview.

Greer and Bessent Set to Meet with China This Weekend

On Saturday, Greer and Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent will meet with China Vice Premier He Lifeng in Switzerland. Greer believes that the meeting will act as a stepping stone for more advanced discussions between the two world powers.

The market is responding positively to the first trade deal with the S&P 500 (SPX) up by 1.28%.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Dow Jones Index Today: DIA Surges Higher on U.S.-UK Trade Deal, China Trade Deal Optimism
data.description:
The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) is rising higher after President Trump officially announced a trade deal with the UK. The terms of the deal include lower U.S. tariffs on UK vehicles, the establishment of a aluminum and steel trading zone, and the elimination of several non-tariff barriers in the UK. In addition, the U.S. will maintain its 10% tariff on the UK.

After announcing the deal, Trump made several optimistic statements on China ahead of the U.S.-China meeting this weekend. He said that the 145% tariffs on the world’s second-largest economy won’t go any higher, adding that a meeting with China President Xi Jinping was possible if the weekend meeting goes well.

The Dow Jones is up by 1.30% on the day, bringing its weekly return to 0.96% and softening its year-to-date loss to 3.36%.

Which Stocks are Moving the Dow Jones?

Let’s take a look at TipRank’s Dow Jones Heatmap, which illustrates the stocks that have contributed to the index’s price action.

Strength in the technology and financial sectors are giving a big helping hand to the Dow Jones’ gains. At the same time, all four healthcare stocks in the index are in the red.

DIA Stock Moves Higher with the Dow Jones

The SPDR Dow Jones Industrial Average ETF (DIA) is an exchange-traded fund designed to track the movement of the Dow Jones. As such, DIA is rising in correlation with the Dow Jones today.

Wall Street believes that DIA stock has room to rise. During the past three months, analysts have issued an average DIA price target of $468.17 for the stocks within the index, implying upside of 12.38% from current prices. The 31 stocks in the DIA carry 29 buy ratings, 2 hold ratings, and zero sell ratings.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Trump Teases Meeting with China President Xi Jinping, Caps Tariffs at 145%
data.description:
Speaking from the Oval Office after announcing the U.S.-UK trade deal, President Trump said that a meeting with China President Xi Jinping was possible if the meeting between the two countries this weekend goes well. Trump also added that the 145% tariffs on China won’t get any higher.

U.S Set to Meet with China on Saturday

Over the weekend, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer will meet with Chinese Vice Premier He Lifeng in Switzerland. Ahead of the meeting, China Foreign Ministry spokesman Lin Jian wrote in an X post that “China is open to dialogue, but any dialogue must be based on equality, respect and mutual benefit.”

The iShares China Large-Cap ETF (FXI) is trading higher on the news and is now up by 1.57% on the day.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Trump: UK Trade Deal to Create $6 Billion in External Revenue
data.description:
President Trump has officially announced a trade deal with the UK, stating that the agreement will create $6 billion in external revenue and $5 billion in revenue for U.S exporters. In addition, the U.S. will have “unprecedented access to UK market,” which includes ethanol, beef, cereals, vegetables, and more.

U.S. and UK to Continue Working on Other Aspects of Trade Deal

Furthermore, the two countries will create an aluminum and steel trading zone. The UK has also agreed to a $10 billion Boeing (BA) procurement deal.

In the meantime, both sides will continue working on other aspects of the deal, such as reaching an agreement on pharmaceutical trade and the remaining reciprocal tariffs.

The S&P 500 (SPX) is trading higher on the developments.

Disclaimer & DisclosureReport an Issue
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Trump to Reduce UK Car Imports to 10%, Beef to Near Zero
data.description:
President Trump is set to slash tariffs on imported UK vehicles to 10% as part of a one-year “temporary arrangement,” according to a new report from The Telegraph. British automakers will be given a quota of 100,000 vehicles that will be subject to the lower tariffs. The arrangement is expected to preclude a comprehensive trade deal between the two nations.

In addition, both countries will almost completely eliminate tariffs on beef trade. The U.S. will also reportedly reduce its tariff on UK-made steel, although the exact percentage is still unclear.

The S&P 500 (SPX) is trading higher on the news.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: J.P. Morgan Slashes China's GDP Outlook on "Higher-than-Expected U.S. Tariffs"
data.description:
The storm brewing between the U.S. and China just got fiercer. J.P. Morgan's (JPM) Haibin Zhu made it clear: "Higher-than-expected U.S. tariffs will drag China's GDP by 0.7%." This isn't just a warning shot—it's a stark prediction of the economic pressures building on China's already volatile market. The impact is real, and it's shaking up Chinese stocks. But how will this all play out for investors?

U.S. Tariffs Drag China's Growth Outlook Down

With new U.S. tariffs hitting Chinese goods, China's growth forecast has been slashed. As Zhu points out, the rising tariffs could shave off 0.7% from China's GDP. The ramifications are undeniable. The country's strong start to 2025, including a 5% GDP growth target, is now threatened. Exports are feeling the heat, and domestic consumption remains sluggish. This pressure is making waves across Chinese stocks, with companies like Alibaba (BABA) and Tencent (TCEHY) showing signs of strain. These giants are now navigating not just trade wars but tightening government policies and reduced global demand.

China Responds Rapidly with Stimulus and Policy Shifts

In response, China is ramping up its fiscal stimulus. With an additional 1 trillion yuan bond issuance, the government is trying to get domestic consumption back on track. In the short term, however, experts like Zhu argue that these measures may not be enough to offset the damage from trade tensions. “While we do not think CNY depreciation will be used as a policy response to tariff risk,” Zhu says, “tolerating a slight yuan weakness is likely.” In fact, this approach is already evident in the market, where the yuan has seen some weakness.

Chinese Stocks Face Uncertainty Amid Trade Wars

Investors now have to brace for more uncertainty in Chinese stocks. As the geopolitical landscape shifts, companies in export-heavy sectors like JD.com (HK:9618) and Baidu (BIDU) could face more challenges. Analysts predict that the ripple effect from U.S. tariffs could drag the broader market down. So, is this a time to buy, sell, or hold? The key is understanding the shifting dynamics.

Using tools like TipRanks' Stock Comparison tool, you can track how analysts are adjusting their forecasts for Chinese stocks in real-time.

data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: India-Pakistan Tensions May Have Slowed Down U.S-India Trade Deal
data.description:
Ongoing tensions between India and Pakistan may have contributed to a trade deal lag between the U.S. and India, according to Fox Business senior correspondent Charles Gasparino. Gasparino, citing Wall Street executives with ties to the White House, added that this contributed to the U.S. first announcing a trade deal with the UK.

Trump to Announce U.S-UK Trade Deal

Gasparino also noted that trade deals with South Korea and Japan are said to be “moving along.” President Trump is set to announce a trade deal with the UK at 10:00 AM ET.

The S&P 500 (SPX) is trading higher ahead of the news.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: U.S. Jobless Claims Fall to 228,000, Beating Expectations
data.description:
The Labor Department has released unemployment benefit data for the week ended May 3, reporting that U.S. jobless claims totaled 228,000. That figure was better than the expectation for 230,000 claims. A week ago, jobless claims tallied in at 241,000.

U.S. Jobless Claims Ahead of Expectations

Furthermore, continuing unemployment claims for the week ended April 26 tallied in at 1.88 million compared to 1.91 million a week before. The data lags U.S. jobless claims by one week.

Investors can keep up with other key economic data using TipRank’s Economic Indicators Dashboard.

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 5/8/25 – Futures Surge on Potential U.S.-U.K. Trade Deal
data.description:
U.S. stock futures saw a major uptrend on Thursday, as positive sentiment was fueled by reports that the White House will soon announce a trade agreement with the U.K. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) were up 1.26%, 0.65%, and 0.89%, respectively, at 4:53 a.m. EST, May 8.

Simultaneously, President Donald Trump clarified his stance on trade negotiations with China, stating that he would not lower tariffs as a precondition for talks scheduled to take place in Switzerland this weekend.

Also, the Federal Reserve decided to maintain interest rates yesterday. However, Fed Chair Jerome Powell warned about rising inflation and job losses but dismissed early rate cut possibilities due to potential tariff impacts. His comments showed the Fed’s priority is to keep the economy stable.

In Wednesday’s regular trading session, the Dow Jones, the S&P 500, and the Nasdaq Composite (NDAQ) rose 0.7%, 0.43%, and 0.27%, respectively.

The upside came due to reports that the U.S. plans to ease AI chip export restrictions helped fuel optimism among investors. The policy shift, led by the Trump administration, aims to scrap Biden-era AI chip curbs and replace them with simpler regulations. This move could benefit chipmakers like Nvidia (NVDA), which saw its stock climb 3.1% following the news.

In other corporate news, AppLovin’s (APP) shares soared 13% in after-hours trading, due to the company’s strong earnings results and a deal to divest its mobile gaming division. In contrast, Arm Holdings (ARM) dropped 9% after its latest guidance fell short of Wall Street estimates.

Looking ahead, investors are focused on key economic data points, such as Weekly Jobless Claims, U.S. Productivity, and Wholesale Inventories.

Further, the busy earnings week continues with reports expected today from ConocoPhillips (COP) and Warner Bros. Discovery (WBD), SoundHound (SOUN), Crocs (CROX), Coinbase (COIN), Pinterest (PINS), DraftKings (DKNG), D-Wave Quantum (QBTS), Rocket Lab (RKLB), Lyft (LYFT), and Paramount Global (PARA).

Meanwhile, the U.S. 10-year treasury yield was up, floating near 4.298%. Simultaneously, WTI crude oil futures are trending higher, hovering near $58.39 per barrel as of the last check.

At the same time, the Gold Spot U.S. Dollar Price declined to $3,340 per ounce on Thursday, due to speculation about a potential U.S. trade deal.

Elsewhere, European indices opened higher today as investors watched central bank rate decisions and corporate earnings. Also, optimism grew around U.K.-U.S. trade relations, with reports suggesting Britain may be the first country to secure a deal following new U.S. tariffs.

Asia-Pacific Markets Ended Higher

Asia-Pacific indices were in the green today after President Trump announced plans to disclose a trade deal with a major country, reportedly the U.K.

At the same time, the Hong Kong index was up 0.37%. Further, China's Shenzhen Component and Shanghai Composite indices closed higher by 0.93% and 0.28%, respectively. Also, Japan's Topix and Nikkei indices gained 0.1% and 0.41%, respectively.

Interested in more economic insights? Tune in to our LIVE webinar.

data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Trump to Unveil 'Major Trade Deal' with UK After Tariff Blitz
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump is expected to announce a trade deal with the UK on Thursday, according to media reports. This would make the UK the first country to secure an agreement following the White House’s recent tariffs. The news follows a social media post by Trump, in which he teased the announcement of a “major trade deal” with a “big and highly respected country.”

Earlier, a UK official confirmed on Tuesday that both countries have made substantial progress toward a trade deal, which is expected to include reduced tariff quotas on steel and automobiles. Notably, UK car and steel exports to the U.S. are currently subject to a 25% tariff.

Meanwhile, the Trump administration had previously confirmed that it is actively negotiating trade deals with India, the UK, South Korea, and Japan.

Below are some of the UK stocks affected by Trump’s tariffs.
